beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and get ready for the day at home.
beats.reasoning: Henry starts every day with a swim, but this beat is the morning wake-up routine. He'll head to the pool immediately after, so folding this into a quick 45-min home beat sets up the day.

beats.activity: Morning swim at the community pool.
beats.reasoning: Henry can't start the day without a swim. Local pool is within walking distance of home, so a 55-min swim session (including changing) fits his morning ritual perfectly before work.

beats.activity: Commute to work on Capitol Hill via bus and walking.
beats.location_name: First Hill Elementary School (First Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Light Rail is closed 9am–3pm, so Henry takes the bus from Beacon Hill to First Hill. 35 min allows for walking to the bus stop and transit time without rushing.

beats.activity: Counseling sessions and meetings with students and teachers at school.
beats.location_name: First Hill Elementary School (First Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Henry's workday begins. He's scheduled for morning counseling sessions and staff coordination. The 11am FIFA match is happening, but as a school counselor on duty, he won't attend; this is a normal work beat.

beats.activity: Lunch break at a nearby café.
beats.reasoning: Henry takes a lunch break near school. He's frugal, so he likely brought lunch or grabs something affordable and quick rather than splurging on the downtown FIFA bar scene.

beats.activity: Afternoon counseling sessions, student check-ins, and administrative work.
beats.location_name: First Hill Elementary School (First Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Afternoon work block continues. The Light Rail closure doesn't affect him since he's already at work. He stays focused on his counseling duties rather than taking advantage of the FIFA match.

beats.activity: Commute home via bus, arriving in Beacon Hill.
beats.reasoning: Henry leaves work and heads home. Light Rail closure has ended by now (3pm), but he sticks with the bus. 45 min accounts for transit and walking from the bus stop.

beats.activity: Stop at the community garden to tend to vegetable beds.
beats.location_name: Beacon Hill Community Garden (Beacon Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: On a sunny Monday, Henry indulges his passion for urban gardening. The garden is within his neighborhood, so it's a quick walk. He checks on plants and does light maintenance.

beats.activity: Walk home and prepare an early dinner.
beats.reasoning: Henry heads home to cook. Being frugal, he likely uses fresh ingredients from his garden or a budget-friendly grocery store. Dinner is early since he values a relaxed evening.

beats.activity: Salsa dancing class at a local studio.
beats.location_name: Beacon Hill Salsa Studio (Beacon Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Henry spends free time on salsa dancing. On a beautiful June evening, he heads to his regular class. This is a favorite activity and fits his social but selective personality (small class over big crowds).

beats.activity: Walk home and unwind with a book or podcast.
beats.reasoning: Henry returns home post-dance, energized but ready to wind down. He relaxes with low-key entertainment before bed, staying true to his preference for calm evenings.
Henry goes to bed at a reasonable hour after a full, active day. He gets 10 hours of sleep, waking up refreshed and ready for another swim-filled morning.

diary:
Started the day right with an early swim at Beacon Hill Pool—the water was perfect, and I needed it after a restless night. The commute to First Hill was smooth, and I spent the morning with back-to-back counseling sessions. One kid, Marcus, finally opened up about his parents' divorce. Those moments remind me why I do this.

Café Solstice had my usual oat milk latte waiting. I caught snippets of the USA-Wales match on someone's phone—the whole city felt buzzy about it, bars already packed downtown even though I was nowhere near that scene.

Afternoon flew by with more check-ins and paperwork. By the time I got home, I needed to move. The community garden was calling—my tomato plants are actually thriving this year. Then salsa class was exactly what I needed; Miguel had us working on new footwork and I actually nailed it for once. Felt good to dance out the day's weight.
thought_process: I was deliberate about the swim this morning—needed that reset before facing a full day of emotional labor. The World Cup buzz downtown didn't really pull at me; I'm not a huge sports person, and I knew the bars would be chaos. Instead, I leaned into what actually grounds me: the garden, the dancing, the small wins with kids like Marcus. The Light Rail closure didn't affect my route, so I didn't stress about it. I think my mood shifted once I got moving in the afternoon—counseling is draining, and I noticed I was craving physical activity and community. The salsa class was the right call; it reminded me there's joy outside the school building.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
